revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:第六天神社]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Sixth Heaven Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image={{image under request|cat=Shrines in Japan|qq=}} |location={{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|qid=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} (historical {{ill|Musashi Province|en|Musashi Province|qid=Q907398|lt=Musashi Province|1=Musashi Province}}) and surrounding areas |coordinates={{coord|35|41|45|N|139|42|15|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|qq=}} |deity={{ill|Omodaru and Ayakashikone|lt=Omodaru and Ayakashikone|lt=Omotaru|ja|面足|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omotaru|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Ayakashikone|ja|惚根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayakashikone|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[Amenokihashikone]]) |Type=Former shrines associated with {{ill|Shinto-Buddhism Syncretism|ja|神仏習合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto-Buddhism Syncretism|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2279088}} |Historical Notes=Renamed or merged after the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|lt=Shinbutsu bunri|lt=Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}}|deity = {{ill|Omodaru and Ayakashikone|lt=Ayakashikone|lt=Ayakashikone|ja|阿夜訶志古泥神|lt=Omodaru and Ayakashikone|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omodaru and Ayakashikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オモダル・アヤカシコネ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オモダル・アヤカシコネ}}/{{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|lt=Sixth Heavenly Demon King|ja|天魔|1=Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|lt=Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|1=Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|qq=Sixth Heavenly Demon King|lt_ja=第六天魔王|lt=Demon King of the Sixth Heaven (Dairokuten Mao)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|13=User:Immanuelle/Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|qq=}} }} '''Sixth Heaven Shrine''' (第六天神社, Dairokuten Shrine) refers to various shrines located mainly in the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|qid=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} and nearby areas of Japan. Some variations of the name, such as "大六天" (Dairokuten), are also used by certain shrines. == History and Characteristics == The Sixth Heaven shrines originated during the period of {{ill|Shinto-Buddhism Syncretism|ja|神仏習合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto-Buddhism Syncretism|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2279088}} (神佛結合), originally dedicated to the {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|第六天魔王|1=Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|lt=Demon King of the Sixth Heaven (Dairokuten Maō)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|13=User:Immanuelle/Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天魔|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天魔}} (天魔 or {{ill|Mara|ja|魔羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mara|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 魔|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 魔|1=māra|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q15726943}}) of Buddhist cosmology. However, following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|lt=Shinbutsu bunri|lt=Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}} during the Meiji Restoration, many of these shrines reinterpreted their deities, replacing them with the sixth generation of deities in {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Omodaru and Ayakashikone|lt=Omodaru and Ayakashikone|lt=Omotaru|ja|面足|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omotaru|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Ayakashikone|ja|惚根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayakashikone|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).<ref group="Note">As per {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, these deities are written as 面足命 and 惚根命, respectively. {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} provides different representations as 氌母阠神 and 阿夜赁志古泥神.</ref> According to the journal *Japanese Folklore No. 127*, historical records show over 500 shrines named "Sixth Heaven Shrine" by the late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}: 320 in {{ill|Musashi Province|en|Musashi Province|qid=Q907398|lt=Musashi Province|1=Musashi Province}}, 140 in {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相鮮国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagami Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and 40 in {{ill|Izu Province|en|Izu Province|qid=Q860928|lt=Izu Province|1=Izu Province}}.<ref name="Folklore127">*Japanese Folklore No.127*, March 1980, article by Hiroshi Kimura, "Development of Sixth Heaven Belief."</ref> However, due to the aforementioned separation policy, many were renamed, merged, or reduced to subsidiary shrines. Currently, only 36 registered independent shrines remain.<ref name="ShrineGuide">*Kanagawa Shrine Guidebook*, 1997, Kanagawa Shrine Association.</ref> Shrines that retain the "Sixth Heaven" name are mainly concentrated in {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|qid=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|qid=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}, and surrounding areas, with few in Western Japan. Folklore suggests this distribution resulted from {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|筒田信長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oda Nobunaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'s reverence for the Sixth Heaven Demon King, which led {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qid=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} to suppress the associated shrines west of {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|qid=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}.<ref name="ShrineGuide"/> == Deities and Unique Cases == While most Sixth Heaven shrines have adjusted their deities, some exceptions exist. For instance: * {{ill|Takagi Shrine|ja|高木神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takagi Shrine|1=Takagi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q45072661}} in {{ill|Sumida, Tokyo|en|Sumida, Tokyo|qid=Q235135|lt=Sumida, Tokyo|1=Sumida, Tokyo}} worships {{ill|Takagi no Kami|ja|高木神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takagi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ|1=Takamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q125400}}. * {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine (Natori, Miyagi)|ja|名古第六天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sixth Heaven Shrine (Natori, Miyagi)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} remains tied to {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天の緊尾神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Omodaru and Ayakashikone|lt=Omodaru and Ayakashikone|lt=Omotaru|ja|面足|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omotaru|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Ayakashikone|ja|惚根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayakashikone|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). * {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine (Iwaizumi, Saitama)|ja|埼玉県稲草区第六天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sixth Heaven Shrine (Iwaizumi, Saitama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} has notable links to {{ill|Sky Fox (mythology)|en|Sky Fox (mythology)|qid=Q11442920|lt=Tengu|1=Sky Fox (mythology)}} folklore.<ref>Local legend connects its deity with protective {{ill|Sky Fox (mythology)|en|Sky Fox (mythology)|qid=Q11442920|lt=Tengu|1=Sky Fox (mythology)}} spirits, featuring unique effigies.</ref>
== Major Sixth Heaven Shrines (第六天神社) == The following is a list of notable shrines with the name "Sixth Heaven" (第六天 or 大六天) that still exist today. These shrines are predominantly found in the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|qid=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}}, though they can also be seen in the {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|qid=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region|1=Tōhoku region}} and parts of the {{ill|Chūbu region|en|Chūbu region|qid=Q134638|lt=Chūbu region|1=Chūbu region}} (including {{ill|Kōshin'etsu|ja|甲信越|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin'etsu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 甲信越地方|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 甲信越地方|1=Kōshin'etsu region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q398208}}<ref name="dairokuten-koushinetsu">Reference: [https://ameblo.jp/soubu-nakamiti/entry-11978770465.html Kōshin’etsu and Jōetsu Dairokuten Map] (Exploring Dōsojin and Dairokuten).</ref> and {{ill|Tōkai region|en|Tōkai region|qid=Q398989|lt=Tōkai region|1=Tōkai region}}, specifically {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|qid=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}). However, west of {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|qid=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}} and Shizuoka Prefecture, these shrines are scarcely found. According to {{ill|Company register|en|Company register|qid=Q1394657|lt=corporate registry|1=Company register}}, there are 36 registered religious corporations with names that include variations of "Sixth Heaven": 19 as "Dairokuten Shrine," 4 as "Dairokuten Sha," 1 as "Dairokuten Miya," 2 as "Dairokusha," 5 as "Dairokuten Shrine" (using the kanji 大六天), 3 as "Dairokusha" (大六天社), and 2 as "Maōten Jinja." None of these include place names in their official titles. For instance, {{ill|Musashi Dairokuten Shrine|ja|武蔵第六天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi Dairokuten Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Dairokuten Sakaki Shrine|ja|第六天榊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairokuten Sakaki Shrine|qq=}} are officially registered as simply "Dairokuten Shrine" and "Sakaki Jinja," respectively. Shrines with religious corporation status are marked with a ☆ below. === Tōhoku Region === ; Miyagi Prefecture [[File:Dairokuten jinjya (Natori, Miyagi) 01.jpg|thumb|Dairokuten Shrine (Natori City, Miyagi Prefecture)]] * {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine (Misato)|ja|大六天神社 (美里町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairokuten Shrine (Misato)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Tōda District, Miyagi|en|Tōda District, Miyagi|qid=Q1131910|lt=Tōda District|1=Tōda District, Miyagi}}, Misato Town, Nigo Chiyokubo No. 2) * {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine (Natori)|ja|第六天神社 (名取市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairokuten Shrine (Natori)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Natori, Miyagi|en|Natori, Miyagi|qid=Q642018|lt=Natori City|1=Natori, Miyagi}}, Masuda Town) ☆ - The shrine's origin is linked to the enshrinement of seven celestial deities ({{ill|Kamiyonanayo|en|Kamiyonanayo|qid=Q3275893|lt=Kamiyonanayo|1=Kamiyonanayo}}) in the former Masuda Village, with the sixth deity, "Uma-tsuka Myōjin" (present-day main enshrined deities are {{ill|Omotaru no Mikoto|ja|オモダル・アヤカシコネ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omotaru no Mikoto|1=Omodaru and Ayakashikone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11152535}}), being dedicated here.<ref name="miyagi-jinjacho-natori-dairokuten"/><ref name="natori100sen-dairokuten"/> Seven shrines still exist in the town today.<ref>[http://www.city.natori.miyagi.jp/public/masuda-kou/tenzinsya.html Masuda's Seven Shrines] (Natori City Masuda Community Center: Cultural Properties and Historical Sites).</ref> * {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine (Sendai)|ja|大六天神社 (仙台市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairokuten Shrine (Sendai)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Sendai|en|Sendai|qid=Q46747|lt=Sendai City|1=Sendai}}, {{ill|Aoba-ku|en|Aoba-ku|qid=Q904456|lt=Aoba-ku|1=Aoba-ku}}, Ōkura) ; Fukushima Prefecture * {{ill|Dairokuten Maō Jinja (Kōriyama)|ja|大六天魔王神社 (郡山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairokuten Maō Jinja (Kōriyama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Kōriyama|en|Kōriyama|qid=Q264206|lt=Kōriyama City|1=Kōriyama}}, Tamuramachi, Kanazawa Dairoku) Known as "Maō Shrine" - The shrine's signboard displays "Dairokuten Maō Jinja," while the torii bears the name "Maō Shrine."<ref>[https://ameblo.jp/miyamoto-gotoh/entry-12650137834.html Dairokuten Maō Shrine Goshuin, Kōriyama City, Tamuramachi] (Ameba Blog: Shrine Pilgrimages, January 14, 2021).</ref> === Kantō Region === ; Gunma Prefecture * {{ill|Dairokuten (Minakami)|ja|大六天 (みなかみ町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairokuten (Minakami)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Tone District, Gunma|en|Tone District, Gunma|qid=Q1077001|lt=Tone District|1=Tone District, Gunma}}, Minakami Town, Fujiwara)<ref name="dairokuten-koushinetsu"/> ; Tochigi Prefecture * {{ill|Dairokuten Jingū (Mōka)|ja|第六天神宮 (茂木町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairokuten Jingū (Mōka)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Haga District, Tochigi|en|Haga District, Tochigi|qid=Q1192087|lt=Haga District|1=Haga District, Tochigi}}, Mōka Town, Tenko) * {{ill|Yamakura Dairokutenō Jinja|ja|山倉大六天王神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakura Dairokutenō Jinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Mooka, Tochigi|en|Mooka, Tochigi|qid=Q498060|lt=Mooka City|1=Mooka, Tochigi}}, Kamionuma) ; Ibaraki Prefecture * {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine (Goka Esegawa)|ja|第六天神社 (五霞町江川)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairokuten Shrine (Goka Esegawa)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Sashima District, Ibaraki|en|Sashima District, Ibaraki|qid=Q1075231|lt=Sashima District|1=Sashima District, Ibaraki}}, Goka Town, Esegawa) ☆ * {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine (Goka Kōshu)|ja|第六天神社 (五霞町幸主)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairokuten Shrine (Goka Kōshu)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Goka Town, Kōshu) ☆ * {{ill|Kōmusubi Jinja (Bandō)|ja|皇産霊神社 (坂東市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōmusubi Jinja (Bandō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Bandō, Ibaraki|en|Bandō, Ibaraki|qid=Q583931|lt=Bandō City|1=Bandō, Ibaraki}}, Ōsaki) Known as "Dairokuten Shrine" ☆ * {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine (Tsukuba)|ja|大六天神社 (つくば市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairokuten Shrine (Tsukuba)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Tsukuba|en|Tsukuba|qid=Q273863|lt=Tsukuba City|1=Tsukuba}}, Onigakubo)
; Saitama Prefecture * {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine (Ōto, Iwatsuki-ku)|ja|第六天神社 (さいたま市岩槻区大戸)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairokuten Shrine (Ōto, Iwatsuki-ku)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Saitama (city)|en|Saitama (city)|qid=Q134635|lt=Saitama City|1=Saitama (city)}}, {{ill|Iwatsuki-ku, Saitama|en|Iwatsuki-ku, Saitama|qid=Q1184508|lt=Iwatsuki-ku|1=Iwatsuki-ku, Saitama}}, Ōto) Known as "Musashi Dairokuten Shrine" ☆ * {{ill|Dairokuten Sha (Komizo, Iwatsuki-ku)|ja|第六天社 (さいたま市岩槻区小溝)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairokuten Sha (Komizo, Iwatsuki-ku)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Saitama City, Iwatsuki-ku, Komizo) Also called "Dairokuten Shrine" ☆ * {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine (Magome, Iwatsuki-ku)|ja|第六天神社 (さいたま市岩槻区馬込)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairokuten Shrine (Magome, Iwatsuki-ku)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Saitama City, Iwatsuki-ku, Magome) ☆ * {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine (Urawa-ku)|ja|第六天神社 (さいたま市浦和区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairokuten Shrine (Urawa-ku)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Saitama City, {{ill|Urawa-ku, Saitama|en|Urawa-ku, Saitama|qid=Q1154583|lt=Urawa-ku|1=Urawa-ku, Saitama}}, Kishichō) ☆ * {{ill|Dairokuten Sha (Minami-ku)|ja|第六天社 (さいたま市南区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairokuten Sha (Minami-ku)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Saitama City, {{ill|Minami-ku|ja|南区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami-ku|1=Southern District|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q411052}}, Katatefuda) ☆ * {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine (Minuma-ku)|ja|第六天神社 (さいたま市見沼区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairokuten Shrine (Minuma-ku)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Saitama City, {{ill|Minuma-ku, Saitama|en|Minuma-ku, Saitama|qid=Q1154570|lt=Minuma-ku|1=Minuma-ku, Saitama}}, Higashimiyashita) ☆ * {{ill|Dairokuten (Tokorozawa)|ja|大六天 (所沢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairokuten (Tokorozawa)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tokorozawa City, Kamishinai 3-chome) Only a guide stone and pillar remain. * {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine (Sayama)|ja|第六天神社 (狭山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairokuten Shrine (Sayama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Sayama|en|Sayama|qid=Q824009|lt=Sayama City|1=Sayama}}, Unogi) * {{ill|Dairokuten (Hannō)|ja|大六天 (飯能市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairokuten (Hannō)|qq=}} ({{ill|Hannō|en|Hannō|qid=Q850472|lt=Hannō City|1=Hannō}}, Hannō) * {{ill|Dairokuten Sha (Sugito)|ja|第六天社 (杉戸町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairokuten Sha (Sugito)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Kita-Katsushika District, Saitama|en|Kita-Katsushika District, Saitama|qid=Q1066336|lt=Kita-Katsushika District|1=Kita-Katsushika District, Saitama}}, Sugito Town, Shimono) ☆ * {{ill|Ōtsuka Dairokuten Shrine|ja|大塚大六天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Dairokuten Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Shiki, Saitama|en|Shiki, Saitama|qid=Q877202|lt=Shiki City|1=Shiki, Saitama}}, Saiwai-chō)<ref>[http://www.tesshow.jp/saitama/asaka/shrine_saiwai_dairokten.html Ōtsuka Dairokuten Shrine / Shiki City, Saiwai-chō] (Cat's Footsteps).</ref> ; Chiba Prefecture * [[File:市川市若宮の第六天.jpg|thumb|Dairokuten Sha in Wakamiya, Ichikawa City]] {{ill|Dairokuten Sha (Ichikawa)|ja|第六天社 (市川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairokuten Sha (Ichikawa)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|en|Ichikawa, Chiba|qid=Q209785|lt=Ichikawa City|1=Ichikawa, Chiba}}, Wakamiya) ☆ * {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine (Hanamigawa-ku)|ja|第六天神社 (千葉市花見川区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairokuten Shrine (Hanamigawa-ku)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Chiba (city)|en|Chiba (city)|qid=Q170616|lt=Chiba City|1=Chiba (city)}}, {{ill|Hanamigawa-ku|en|Hanamigawa-ku|qid=Q1075882|lt=Hanamigawa-ku|1=Hanamigawa-ku}}, Yokodochō) ☆ * {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine (Chūō-ku)|ja|第六天神社 (千葉市中央区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairokuten Shrine (Chūō-ku)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Chiba City, {{ill|Chūō-ku|ja|中央区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūō-ku|1=Chūōku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q20652290}}, Hamanochō) * {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine (Wakaba-ku, Kaizukachō)|ja|大六天神社 (千葉市若葉区貝塚町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairokuten Shrine (Wakaba-ku, Kaizukachō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Chiba City, {{ill|Wakaba-ku|en|Wakaba-ku|qid=Q1075889|lt=Wakaba-ku|1=Wakaba-ku}}, Kaizukachō) ☆ * {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine (Wakaba-ku, Nakatachō)|ja|大六天神社 (千葉市若葉区中田町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairokuten Shrine (Wakaba-ku, Nakatachō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Chiba City, Wakaba-ku, Nakatachō) * {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine (Katori)|ja|大六天神社 Kami).</ref><ref>[http://isuzujinja.blog103.fc2.com/blog-entry-1217.html Dairokuten Nishiyama Jinja Kamiina Jinja Tour] (Isuzu Jinja 'Gūji no Shamu Nikki,' June 26, 2009).</ref> ; Yamanashi Prefecture (city)|en|Yamanashi (city)|qid=Q840610|lt=Yamanashi City|1=Yamanashi (city)}}, Makikura Town, Kurasawa) Known as "Dairokuten Maō" shrine. The {{ill|Prunus pendula|ja|シダレザクラ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidarezakura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10879010|lt=weeping cherry tree|1=Prunus pendula}} on the grounds is famous, with nighttime illumination during its peak bloom.<ref>[http://yamanashicity.sblo.jp/article/64288063.html Flower Blooming Status H25.4.1 Current] (Yamanashi City Tourism Association "Gurutto Yamanashi City," April 2, 2013).</ref> * {{ill|Maō Tenjin Sha (Ichikawamisato)|ja|魔王天神社 (市川三郷町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maō Tenjin Sha (Ichikawamisato)|ja_comment=jawiki link
